failure_modes.text: Teams waste effort on incremental fixes when one constrained threshold or leverage point is actually controlling the system shift.
failure_modes.source_quote: Works best when the system has resisted many incremental fixes and the real question is where leverage actually lives.
failure_modes.mitigation: Identify the controlling threshold: map which constraint, adoption level, or resource boundary actually governs the system shift, and focus effort there instead of on incremental surface fixes.

failure_modes.text: Breakthrough narratives become fantasy when people romanticize the moment of change and underinvest in the prerequisite buildup it requires.
failure_modes.source_quote: Danger when leaders romanticize breakthrough moments.
failure_modes.mitigation: Invest in the prerequisite buildup: before expecting a breakthrough, verify that the conditions for the tipping point are being systematically constructed, not just hoped for.

failure_modes.text: Teams miss true leverage when they treat visible symptoms as the tipping point instead of diagnosing the deeper constraint.
failure_modes.source_quote: Focusing on symptoms, not root causes.
failure_modes.mitigation: Diagnose beyond symptoms: when looking for leverage, trace visible signals back to the deeper structural constraint that actually determines when the system tips.

premortem_questions.text: What specific variable, condition, or social threshold would need to change for this system to shift disproportionately?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Identify the condition that turns a marginal effort into a self-reinforcing shift.

premortem_questions.text: What would need to be true for this idea to work that does not yet hold today?
premortem_questions.source_quote: What would need to be true for this idea to work?

premortem_questions.text: Which apparent bottleneck is only a symptom, and where does the real leverage live underneath it?
premortem_questions.source_quote: Focus on strategic interventions ... the issues that have the biggest impact.

heuristics.text: Look for the smallest condition change that could produce the biggest qualitative shift.
heuristics.source_quote: A threshold where a small quantitative change leads to a massive, non-linear qualitative shift.

heuristics.text: Use what-would-need-to-be-true analysis to turn an impossible-looking idea into a testable threshold hypothesis.
heuristics.source_quote: Ask the 'What would need to be true for this idea to work?' question.

heuristics.text: Treat breakthrough as the result of leverage plus buildup, not as magic.
heuristics.source_quote: Danger when leaders romanticize breakthrough moments and underinvest in the slow prerequisite buildup.

curation_notes.summary: Kept the chunks on leverage thresholds, what-must-be-true analysis, and anti-breakthrough romanticism.
donor_drops: Did not preserve broader communication and charisma-spike sprawl beyond the clearest threshold logic.
open_questions: The source also supports persuasion and culture-change uses, but the Wave 2 surface stays on leverage and threshold diagnosis.

allies: The raw allies section is strongest on systems-thinking, first-principles-thinking, and leverage-points.
antagonists: The source is strongest against status-quo-bias, representativeness-heuristic, and confirmation-bias.
structured_tensions: The clearest tensions are with status-quo-bias, representativeness-heuristic, and confirmation-bias.
